reasoning: This kernel performs 1 FLOP (multiply) per element while transferring 16 bytes (8-byte double read + 8-byte double write), yielding an arithmetic intensity of 1/16 = 0.0625 FLOP/byte, which is far below the ridge point of 62 FLOP/byte. The kernel is essentially a streaming memory copy with a scalar multiply, so it will saturate DRAM bandwidth while leaving compute units almost entirely idle. With 4096×256 = 1M threads processing 64M elements and a grid-stride loop, there is sufficient parallelism to fully utilize memory bandwidth, confirming high DRAM utilization rather than a latency or occupancy issue.

With 4096×256 = 1M threads processing 64M elements and a grid-stride loop, there is sufficient parallelism to fully utilize memory bandwidth, confirming high DRAM utilization rather than a latency or occupancy issue.
